data.title: GXO Logistics expands partnership with Henkel to include the beauty segment
data.description:
GXO Logistics (NYSE:GXO) announced on Thursday that it has extended and expanded its partnership in France with Henkel (OTCPK:HELKF) (OTCPK:HENKY).

The global contract logistics company manages storage, distribution, seasonal returns and value added-services for an array of Henkel brands across its laundry and home care segments and will now expand its services to include Henkel’s iconic beauty brands.

GXO (GXO) manages logistics for Henkel at its 60,000-square-meter site in the Orléans region of central France. With the addition of the beauty segment, GXO (GXO) will continue to add cutting-edge technology that delivers continued competitive efficiency on site, such as a pick to light solution and an inventory viewer, to support the transformation of picking processes and boost efficiency. GXO (GXO) also provides value-added services with co-packing activities and a transport tower control to serve Henkel’s customers in France as well as in the French overseas. Henkel brands served at the site include Super Croix, Xtra, Le Chat, Persil, Mir, Maison Verte, Decolor Stop, K2r, Catch, Fa, Taft, Palette and Schwarzkopf.

"Our unrivaled logistics experience and expertise will continue to optimize Henkel’s operations and provide enhanced service and quality to its customers. We look forward to deploying additional technologies to further boost efficiency and enhance safety," stated GXO executive Vincent Ricci.

Shares of GXO Logistics (GXO) were up 1% in premarket trading on Thursday to trade at a new 52-week high.

data.title: Blackstone Q3 earnings top consensus as investment activity picks up
data.description:
Blackstone (NYSE:BX) turned in better-than-expected Q3 earnings on Thursday as investment activity picked up, and fund appreciation was its strongest in three years. Among its strategies, corporate private equity and infrastructure realized the best performances during the quarter.

Total assets under management of $1.11T at Sept. 30, 2024, topping the Visible Alpha consensus of $1.09T, increased from $1.08T at June 30. Fee-earning AUM rose to $820.5B from $808.7B at the end of Q2.

Net inflows of $40.5B increased from $39.4B in Q2.

Q3 distributable EPS of $1.01, beating the $0.93 consensus, rose from $0.96 in Q2 and $0.94 in last year's Q3.

Blackstone (BX) stock rose 1.5% in Thursday premarket trading.

"We invested or committed $54B in the quarter — the highest in over two years — and deployed $123B in the last twelve months since the cost of capital peaked," Chairman and CEO Stephen A. Schwarzman said.

Q3 fee-related earnings of $1.18B, or $0.96 per share, increased from $1.11B, or $0.91 per share, in the prior quarter and $1.12B, or $0.92 per share, in the year-ago period.

Net management and advisory fees were $1.79B, up from $1.78B in Q2 and $1.65B in last year's Q3.

At the end of the quarter, Blackstone (BX) had undrawn capital for investment, or dry powder, of $171.6B vs. $181.0B at the end of Q2. During the quarter, the company deployed $34.0B of capital and recorded realizations of $22.7B.

Total expenses of $1.90B rose from $1.63B in the previous quarter and $1.12B a year ago. Compensation and benefit expenses climbed to $1.44B from $1.21B in Q2 and $946.2M in Q3 2023.

Investment performance by segment (appreciation/gross returns):

  • Real Estate — Opportunistic: +1.1%; Core+: -0.5%;
  • Private Equity — Corporate Private Equity: +6.2%; Tactical Opportunities: +4.3%; Secondaries: +0.7%; Infrastructure: +5.5%;
  • Credit & Insurance — Private Credit: +3.6%; Liquid Credit: +2.4%.
  • Multi-Asset Investing — Absolute Return Composite: +2.2%.

Conference call at 9:00 AM ET.

Earlier, Blackstone beats Q3 estimates

data.title: Saia gains after being added to the Jefferies Franchise Picks list
data.description:
Saia (NASDAQ:SAIA) pushed higher in early trading on Thursday after being named a Jefferies Franchise Pick on Thursday.

Analyst Stephanie Moore said that within asset-based trucking, the firm prefers less than truckload names over TL given the higher barriers to entry and more rational pricing environment. Within LTL, SAIA is seen as attractive due to its long runway for continued above-peer revenue growth and margin expansion. Specifically, Moore wrote that SAIA has upside as it builds out its terminal footprint to improve density and closes the wide gap in pricing vs. peers. "Compared to its public peers, SAIA has one of the lowest terminal counts and lowest revenue/shipment metrics —providing a clear path for improvement," she noted. Jefferies sees a path to at least $20 in EPS for SAIA in 2027 through the flywheel of more terminals leading to a more fluid network, better service, better pricing, and higher margins.

Jefferies has a Buy rating and price target of $50 on Saia (SAIA). "At 27x 2025 EPS and 22x 2026 EPS vs. ODFL at >30x and a historic range between 25x-30x, we think shares look attractive considering the oppty for growth in the years ahead," observed Moore.

Shares of Saia (SAIA) were up 0.89% in premarket action on Thursday. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on SAIA is at Hold.

data.title: Huntington Bancshares GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.03, revenue of $1.89B beats by $30M
data.description:
• Huntington Bancshares press release (NASDAQ:HBAN): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.33 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $1.89B (flat Y/Y) beats by $30M.
  • Average total loans and leases increased $1.1 billion, or 1%, from the prior quarter to $124.5 billion, and increased $3.7 billion, or 3%, from the year-ago quarter.
  • Net charge-offs of 0.30% of average total loans and leases for the quarter.
  • Nonperforming asset ratio of 0.62% at quarter end.
  • Allowance for credit losses (ACL) of $2.4 billion, or 1.93% of total loans and leases, at quarter end.

data.title: Iridium Communications beats Q3 top and bottom line estimates, gives FY outlook
data.description:
• Iridium Communications press release (NASDAQ:IRDM): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.21 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $212.8M beats by $6.63M.
  • Total service revenue growth of approximately 5% for full-year 2024. Total service revenue for 2023 was $584.5 million. (Previous guidance was for total service revenue growth between 4% and 6% for full-year 2024.)
  • Full-year 2024 OEBITDA between $465 million and $470 million. OEBITDA for 2023 was $463.1 million. (Previous guidance was for full-year 2024 OEBITDA of between $460 million and $470 million.)

data.title: Blackstone beats Q3 estimates
data.description:

  • GAAP EPS of $1.02.

  • Total Assets Under Management (AUM) of $1,107.6 billion: Fee-Earning AUM of $820.5 billion; Perpetual Capital AUM of $434.7 billion

  • Inflows of $40.5 billion in the quarter and $166.7 billion over the LTM

  • Deployment of $34.0 billion in the quarter and $123.4 billion over the LTM

  • Realizations of $22.7 billion in the quarter and $77.0 billion over the LTM
